content_brief.title: Best Coffee for Cold Brew: What Makes a Great Cold Brew Bean
content_brief.summary: The best cold brew starts with the right bean. Medium to medium-dark roasts, extra coarse grind, and long steeping produce the smoothest, sweetest cup.
content_brief.key_takeaway: Better cold brew starts with the right bean, the right grind, and the right steep time.
content_brief.audience: coffee drinkers who want smoother cold brew at home
content_brief.best_angle: Not every coffee makes great cold brew.

facebook_post.copy: Not every coffee is the right coffee for cold brew. If you want a smooth, sweet, low-acid cup, the bean matters just as much as the brew method. In this guide, we break down the roast level, origin, grind size, and steep time that make the biggest difference. Our top pick is Colombia El Tiple. Read the full guide and brew better cold brew at home.
facebook_post.link_strategy: Include the article link directly in the post: https://hiswordcoffee.com/blogs/news/best-coffee-for-cold-brew

instagram_caption.copy:
Not every coffee makes great cold brew.

For the smoothest, sweetest cup:
• go medium to medium-dark
• grind extra coarse
• steep 16–20 hours

Top pick: Colombia El Tiple.

Save this for your next brew day.
